title: Disappearances in Scholar's Summit
recap: We investigated the disappearances in [[Scholar’s Summit]] and ended up fighting a hag in [[Althea Flameheart]]’s home. [[Weledor]] and [[Gukki]] were taken down, but we managed to get us healed later at [[Windward Hall]]. Afterward, we reported to [[Guildmaster Kaela Ravenshadow]], who told us to meet with [[Headmaster Thalindor Mumblethorn]]. When we reached [[Lorehaven Academy]], we discovered that the [[Mumblerhorn]] had vanished as well. In his office we found signs of a struggle and a hidden tunnel—and from inside it, we heard frantic scurrying.

title: Descent into the Tunnels of Lorehaven
recap: We fought darklings in [[Headmaster Mumblethorn]]’s office, then questioned [[Constella]], only to realize she was innocent. [[Finn]] had a panic attack during it all and heard a strange melody coming from [[Darwin]]. After retrieving our weapons, we chose to descend into the hidden tunnel beneath [[Stormharbor]] without asking anyone for help.

title: The Smuggler's Corpse and Demonic Creatures
recap:
In the tunnels under the city, we followed drag marks rather than eerie moaning.
We found a smuggler’s corpse, ghostly figures trapped in a conversation loop, and [[Finn]] received a disturbing vision before magical light was extinguished.
We fought two demonic creatures, one of which fled, leading us to a cavern with forest debris and a lit wooden house.

title: The Underground Troll and Whelps
recap:
Inside the underground house, we investigated necrotic magic from a stewpot and found two illusion-hidden doors.
Choosing one path, we discovered a troll and whelps, waking us during a stealth attempt.
We killed the creatures but were left hurt and low on resources, uncertain whether to press on.

title: A Merrow in the Merchant's Bazaar
recap: During a storm in the [[Merchant’s Bazaar]], we had captured a merrow for [[Vurel Morridan]].

title: An Unlikely Meeting
recap: We saw [[Lirael Stormcrest]] with [[Maximillion Alastair III]].

title: Developments in Sandhaven and Mumblethorn's Journals
recap:
After repelling invading merrow, we explored personal and plot developments.
We heard of an assassination in [[Sandhaven]], an unlit lighthouse, and newly revealed ruins.
[[Oogway]] and [[Thodian]] learned about journals of memories from [[Headmaster Mumblethorn]].
[[Weledor]]’s performance boosted his ego, and several quests were seeded for future adventures.

title: The Reflective Room and a Planar Pull
recap:
We fought faceless creatures in a reflective room.
[[Weledor]] was pulled into another plane.

title: The Ritual at Sanctuary Hills Asylum
recap:
At the [[Sanctuary Hills Asylum]], we found [[Verner Darkholm]] conducting a ritual powered by the artifact [[Celemîr Teltaur]].
[[Slip]] arrived via a portal and joined the fight.
[[Ingvar Lightforge]] blessed us, weakened [[Verner]] by severing his connection to the artifact, and urged us to attune to it.
[[Thodian]] fell in battle, and [[Ingvar]] transformed [[Neldrion]] into the [[Avatar of Anguished Souls]] before dying.

title: Defeat of Darkholm and the Cursed Artifact
recap:
We defeated [[Verner Darkholm]] and [[The Taskmaster]], with [[Weledor]] attuning to [[Celemîr Teltaur]].
We rescued [[Ingvar Lightforge]] and escaped the collapsing asylum, carrying [[Thodian]]’s body in hopes of resurrection.

title: The Motley Maverick's Carnival Crucible
recap:
In [[Madam Yzmarr]]’s tent, repeated tarot results hinted at a weakening protection.
Investigating [[Ruxana Krakendrain]]’s missing ale, we fought corrosive oozes and restored [[Ruxana]]’s true self by speaking her full name.
Taken to [[The Motley Maverick]]’s carnival crucible, we fought sprites, pixies, quicklings, and dryads (some revealed as polymorphed workers).
[[Slip]] attacked [[The Motley Maverick]], prompting him to escalate the challenge.

title: Memories in the Mirror Maze
recap:
We learned of [[Clan Eyanfest]]’s involvement with [[The Mirror Knight]].
Psychic screams prevented details about origins.
In the mirror maze, [[Thodian]] touched a memory of his brother’s abduction and [[Malachite]]’s attack on [[Azorian]].
[[Oogway]] nearly died probing another memory.
We fought phase spiders, with [[Oogway]] poisoned again, before meeting [[Grimscale]].

title: Twilight's Cradle and The Mirror Knight
recap:
[[Grimscale]], a redcap bound to aid [[Thodian]], revealed the realm was [[Twilight’s Cradle]].
Opening cocoons, he freed faun [[Dancer]], who gave us a locket tied to [[The Mirror Knight]]’s true name.
We touched a ceremonial sword and entered a memory revealing [[The Mirror Knight]]’s past, her connection to [[Luthain]], and a scene involving [[The Shattered King]], [[Arcane Confidant]], and [[Fractured Prince]].

title: Dorian's Revelation and The Echoing Bard
recap:
In [[The Mirror Knight]]’s lair, [[Thodian]] dueled her while [[Slip]] fought [[Grimscale]].
Breaking mirrors aided [[Thodian]].
Convincing her to yield, we learned [[Dorian]] lived, that [[The Shattered King]] should be saved, and that answers lay with [[The Echoing Bard]].
[[Weledor]]’s song angered her, prompting this clue.
We left to find a much older [[Dorian]] waiting.

title: Gnoll Attack in Redhill
recap:
After hearing the dire warning from [[The Oracle]], we heard an explosion and howling from the north of [[Redhill]].
We battled a group of gnolls that initially focused on killing civilians, with each death seemingly giving them more power.
After a few civilians were killed, a deep voice commanded the gnolls, who began attempting to kidnap the survivors.
[[Slip]] fell to the gnolls' attacks but was saved by [[Shinusu Rampart]] before he died.

title: Infiltrating the Redhill Mines
recap:
We defeated the gnolls in the town square of [[Redhill]], stopping one kidnapping but not the other.
During the combat, each party member saw a fiendish entity out of the corner of our eyes, which grew closer with each turn before disappearing.
We heard more screams and howls and headed north to stop the gnolls once and for all.
Finding evidence of more attacks and kidnappings, we scouted the entrance to the mines, where gnolls were focused on clearing debris.
We decided to try a sneak attack to draw the creatures out.
[[Slip]] successfully sneaked in and attacked a gnoll, which let out a loud howl of pain.

title: Visions from the Herald of Despair
recap:
We buried [[Gukki]] in a cairn outside the mine entrance.
Villager [[Nolan]] slipped into the mine, and [[Slip]], following him, was suddenly transported to a balcony in [[Stormharbor]] where he saw a demonic figure, [[The Herald of Tormenauk]], who offered him what his heart desired if he helped free its master, [[Tormenauk]].
[[Slip]] was returned to the mines, and as we followed [[Nolan]]’s trail, each member had a similar vision and offer from [[The Herald of Tormenauk]].
We eventually found our way into deeply buried ruins and descended into us.

title: A Demonic Dog and Mechanical Constructs
recap:
After descending into the ruins, we found [[Zorron]] encased in a resin bubble. [[Shinusu Rampart]] broke the bubble, freeing [[Zorron]], who was suffering from memory loss but remembered who [[Tormenauk]] was.
[[Weledor]] triggered the same trap while questioning [[Zorron]], but [[Slip]] quickly freed him.
[[Kiera]] and [[Harris]] bolted from the room.
[[Weledor]], [[Thodian]], and [[Slip]] chased [[Harris]] into a large room with a giant, non-hostile demonic dog named [[Valkorth]], who charmed both [[Weledor]] and [[Harris]].
Meanwhile, [[Oogway]] returned to the hallway where he was attacked, and [[Shin]], [[Enigma]], and [[Zorron]] heard mechanical movement from the room above us.

title: The Specter and the Mechanical Constructs
recap:
After [[Harris]] bolted from the chamber with [[Valkorth]], [[Oogway]] followed closely, with [[Slip]] and [[Weledor]] catching up.
We encountered [[Nolan]], who was acting strangely under the influence of a specter.
Meanwhile, [[Shin]], [[Zorron]], and [[Enigma]] fought and defeated several mechanical constructs.
During the chaos, the other villager (a merchant) seized the opportunity to venture deeper into the ruins.

title: Kez’raeth and Two Suns
recap:
[[Weledor]] was snapped out of his trance when [[Oogway]] was struck by one of [[Nolan]]'s arrows.
Their efforts to reason with [[Nolan]] were interrupted when [[Kez’raeth, the Iron-Hearted]], stormed in and cleaved [[Nolan]] in half with a single strike.
Realizing we were outmatched, [[Weledor]], [[Zorron]], and [[Shin]] retreated, blocking the entrance to the ruins with a large boulder.
Exiting the mine, we were greeted by an unsettling sight: the sky had two suns.

title: Umbryss and the Unraveling World
recap:
[[Zorron]] and [[Enigma]] were overwhelmed by memories.
[[Zorron]] identified the second sun as [[Umbryss]], a harbinger of doom, and recalled his hunt for the [[Shards of Maltheris]].
As [[Umbryss]] faded, his memories fell back into a haze.
The earth rumbled, and spiraling runes erupted from the ground, spreading out from the ruins.
In a terrifying transformation, a herd of deer shifted into fiendish creatures, writhing and screaming in agony.

title: A Race Against Time to Azorian's Tower
recap:
Realizing the villagers were likely freeing [[Tormenauk]], we rushed back to [[Redhill]].
We noticed the flora dying where the runes spread.
The people of [[Redhill]] appeared dazed, some engaging in emotionless fights.
We gathered [[Zarina]] and our coach driver, [[Balin Stonefoot]], from the [[Crimson Hearth Inn]] and decided to leave for [[Azorian's Tower]] to seek his aid.
We learned that [[Josh]] had left town with [[Valdus]] of the [[Shadow Blades]], and the [[Oracle]] later revealed he had not left willingly.

title: The Oracle's Cryptic Warnings
recap:
[[The Oracle]] followed us, interacting with each party member.
She found it odd she couldn’t see [[Zorron]], noted [[Thodian]] seemed split in two, and told [[Slip]] he wasn’t ready for what she had to say.
To [[Weledor]], she apologized for his burdens and praised his music.
She had a longer conversation with [[Oogway]], revealing his and [[Princess Aeliana]]'s lives were linked.
He accepted her help, which accelerated his poisoning but allowed him to see through the eyes of a [[Shadow Blade]], confirming their infiltration of the [[Coral Guard]].
On our seven-day journey to [[Azorian's Tower]], we discovered [[Thodian]] had been infected with the same poison, and he briefly inhabited [[Dorian]]'s body in [[Twilight's Cradle]].

title: The Fall of Azorian's Tower
recap:
We arrived at [[Azorian's Tower]], finding the [[Coral Guard]] camped outside, unable to enter.
[[Oogway]] joined the [[Guard]], hoping to expose the [[Shadow Blades]] and protect the royal family.
As the others searched for a way in, [[Weledor]]'s [[Celemîr Teltaur]] pulled him toward a door that opened to an old man.
We entered, and the old man instructed [[Weledor]] to place the [[Celemîr Teltaur]] on a pedestal.
He then accused us of spying due to our [[Silver Masks]] tattoos, and he and the artifact were blasted into atoms, causing the tower to crumble.
Falling into a hidden chamber, we saw six statues and a seated man.
[[Zorron]] could hear whispers from the statues. [[Thodian]] touched a statue of [[Orthon]], was briefly ensnared, and broke free, revealing a tombstone.
[[Slip]] touched a statue of a woman, causing it to vanish and revealing a leather cloak.
[[Weledor]] repeated a despairing song from a bard's statue, which then changed its expression.
[[Zorron]] healed a dwarf statue, which crumbled to reveal a tombstone.
With all four statues dealt with, the seated man stood, revealed as a younger version of the old man, then collapsed to his knees, weeping as a door opened fully.
We moved through the door.

title: Eyanfest's Might and the Prophecies of Darwin
recap:
In [[Azorian]]'s study, we grew frustrated with his lack of response.
[[Thodian]] wrote down [[Finn]]'s translated messages from [[Darwin]]: 1. [[Galadon]]'s army has gathered at the gates, 2. The walls of [[X'yrath]] have fallen, 3. The ember seat sits empty, 4. The veil is failing, 5. The cult of the voidwalker has discovered the key.
[[Azorian]] read the parchment, his eyes becoming more focused as an animated suit of armor, [[Eyanfest's Might]], challenged [[Thodian]] to a duel.
[[Slip]] found self-writing journals for major cities, each with updates.
He saw reports of: a 'sky has broken' message and missing caravans in [[Sandhaven]]; unregistered ships, missing people, and a creature in [[Harborwatch]]; creeping corruption and memory loss in [[Silvershade]]; strange monk behavior and a glowing ship in [[Tidewater]]; and [[Henrietta Haulblood]]'s disappearance, [[Elaria Starfall]]'s unconsciousness, and the rumored return of the [[Crimson Cartel]] in [[Stormharbor]].

title: A Palace Crumbles, A Glimpse of the Future
recap:
Right after the chaos following [[Grom Blackthorn]]’s defeat. The palace in [[Tidewater]] began collapsing around us, survival was the immediate priority. We snatched up [[Grom]]’s staff, a mysterious pendant, a book, and a pouch of magical seeds, wisely choosing to deal with us later. Our escape route was a desperate dive into the throne room’s central pool—a passage [[Oogway]] knew led to the sea.
The journey through the flooded tunnel was a shock; it was clearly meant for creatures at home in the deep. Mid-journey, we stumbled upon a hidden, dry cavern—a surprisingly intact resting place that suggested it belonged to none other than [[Azorian]]. Taking a much-needed breather, the weight of our situation became clear: a long swim ahead and only one party member truly equipped for it. Yet, [[Weledor]]’s [[Celemîr Teltaur]] began to hum, subtly pulling us deeper into the cavern, guiding our next steps. This led us to a truly immense, flattened-egg-shaped cavern. In its heart, a gigantic [[Celemîr Teltaur]] spun, suspended between colossal stalactites and stalagmites.

A strategic debate ensued. [[Weledor]] feared his own corrupted anchor might taint the massive one, while [[Oogway]] worried about his worsening infection from the [[Widow Pearls]]. Boldly, [[Slip]], [[Shin]], and [[Zorron]] volunteered to attune to the great anchor, deciding to ascend and leaving [[Oogway]], [[Weledor]], and [[Thodian]] below. As the three ascended, we encountered unsettling passageways that opened to what appeared to be visions of other places. One showed the bloody streets of [[Harborwatch]], littered with dwarven bodies and patrolled by ominous black-armored soldiers. Another depicted [[Tidewater]] as it utterly collapsed into ruin under [[Blackthorn]]'s corrupting influence. Reaching the summit, [[Slip]] and [[Shin]] jumped across the final platforms to the gigantic [[Celemîr Teltaur]]. [[Shin]] warned [[Slip]] of the raw, immense power emanating from it, describing it as 'being in the sea during a raging storm.' Undeterred, [[Slip]] touched the anchor. In an instant, [[Slip]] was ripped from the cavern and transported to a pristine college in a place called [[Cyr'dymvyr]], a place he'd never heard of. There he learned shocking truths: the year was **1747**, over 1300 years in the future, and [[Azorian]] had vanished over 400 years ago. Before he could ask more, [[Slip]] was pulled back to the cavern as the enormous [[Celemîr Teltaur]] dramatically shrunk to the size of a medallion.

[[Oogway]], [[Thodian]], and [[Weledor]] witnessed the anchor's transformation and made our way down, passing two more disturbing visions. One showed an empty street in what we recognized as [[Stormharbor]]. The other revealed a bright, sunny city, eerily peaceful but for a few bodies covered in sand. We reunited on the cavern's lower level, the miniaturized [[Celemîr Teltaur]] now in [[Slip]]’s possession. [[Thodian]] was captivated by the portal showing the 'sunny city,' wanting to investigate it further as the others reached the bottom.

title: A Waystation Between Worlds
recap:
We finished solving the problems in [[The Waystation Between Worlds]] and did our 'shopping'.

[[Thodian]] was tempted to enter a one-way door to [[Twilight's Cradle]], received a hint about [[Eyanfest's Might]].
We suspected that [[Xylos]] is [[Mindy]]'s brother and that he is from [[Twilight's Cradle]].
We learned from the devil, who is magically forced to tell one truth a day, that [[Azorian]] had summoned him, tricked him into helping create a powerful magic item, and then imprisoned him.
[[Oogway]] learned that the cure for his [[Void Poison]] is in [[Obsidian Spire]], but that he may need to sacrifice himself to save [[Princess Aeliana Seastone]].
[[Thodian]] was given a one-time teleportation rod to return to the [[Waystation]].
We were warned about waking [[Khatep]].
[[Weledor]] received [[Songweaver]].
[[Zorron]] received [[Mirror's Edge]].

title: The Fall of Sandhaven
recap:
We arrived in [[Sandhaven]], where we were kicked out of [[Ashyra]]'s temple by [[Khatep]].
We found [[Inara al-Sahar]] and a group of citizens hiding in a cavern from an incursion.
During introductions, all but [[Thodian]] were compelled to go to the city square, where we witnessed [[Despair]] murder [[Sarif al-Sahar]], the caliph of [[Sandhaven]] and [[Inara]]'s father, and unleash monsters on the citizens.
We, along with [[Inara]], [[Captain Azar]], and about 30 civilians including a group of street children called [[The Dust Rats]], fled back to the safety of the caverns.
[[Oogway]] promised to help [[Inara]] get revenge against [[Despair]].
[[Khatep]] told we we cannot enter the teleportation chamber until we retrieve [[Ashyra]].
[[Inara]] revealed that she has [[Sandhaven]]'s [[Celemîr Teltaur]] and will give it to we if we help her free the city.
[[Slip]] befriended [[Aurel Sunshield]], the 'leader' of [[The Dust Rats]], and swore to help us.
[[Oogway]] was haunted by [[Despair]]’s words about his past failures and his fear of failing [[Princess Aeliana]], but was also inspired by the beauty of [[Sandhaven]].
[[Thodian]] was angered by [[Despair]]'s words about his inability to save [[Dorian]].

title: Hope and Flight in the Caverns
recap:
After retreating to the caverns, we met [[Magnolia]], a healer tending to the wounded. [[Oogway]] spoke with [[Inara al-Sahar]], who acknowledged her new role as queen but deferred the conversation until the city was reclaimed. We helped with the wounded, with [[Magnolia]] taking the lead. [[Weledor]] played music to calm the people and gave his old pan flute to a young [[Dust Rat]] named [[Nali]], who ran off to practice.

[[Thodian]] spoke with [[Halim]], who revealed that the teleportation chamber was actually the lost library of [[Ashyra]]. [[Halim]] suggested we use it to escape, but we explained that [[Khatep]] had locked it, making escape impossible.

Just as we were making a plan to face [[Despair]], flying aberrations attacked the caverns, forcing everyone to flee our supposed sanctuary.

title: A Desperate Flight Through Sandhaven
recap:
Emerging from the caverns beneath [[Sandhaven]], we collapsed the tunnel behind us, cutting off pursuit from the horrors below. We were immediately met with new dangers: shrieking winged horrors in the sky and the howls of approaching monsters.

Surfacing in the city’s outer districts, we found a small group of survivors, including the street kid [[Aurel]] and [[Captain Azar]].
[[Captain Azar]] proposed a plan: fortify the city watch, [[Dira’ al-Waha]], headquarters as a last bastion of defense. We realized that to abandon these survivors was to condemn us.

Moving through the ruined streets, we were attacked by a trio of hellhounds. We defeated the beasts, but the noise attracted more dangers, and four more civilians were lost to flying horrors.

The remaining survivors are exhausted and broken. We now face roughly three more hours of dangerous travel to reach the headquarters—our last hope for safety.
